description: clone creates an extra young downstream salmon for each salmon passing through — every hatchery cycle emits multiple 'homeless' fish instead of one.

2003-jeff-binder.notes: Clone produces one fewer salmon per cycle than the reference — 3 homeless before Hello instead of 4.

2003-cal-henderson.notes: Clone has no effect — a single homeless per cycle instead of the duplicated output the OCaml reference produces.

2012-quin-kennedy.notes: Clone over-multiplies — 6 homeless per cycle instead of the reference's 4.

2017-addison-bean.notes: Panics with `already borrowed: BorrowMutError` on the first FishDown tick — river.rs:237's move_salmon borrows the parent mutably while the tick recursion still holds it, so any multi-level tree crashes before producing output.

2018-martijn-arts.notes: Parser splits on spaces only, so the newline + indent structure of the source collapses into a flat tree — the intended river layout is lost and output is either empty or a stream of literal node-name salmon.
